text: If an AED is available but does not have child pads or child energy settings, what should you do for a six-year-old non-responsive victim?

options.text: A) Perform CPR but do not use the AED
B) Use the AED but only apply one pad
C) Use the AED in adult mode

explanation: If an AED is not equipped for pediatric use, it is safer to perform CPR and not use the AED at all for a child, as using adult settings or pads could deliver a potentially harmful electrical shock.
